intro: Calamaria coromandelina, also known as Calamari, is a tropical marine algae species belonging to the family Sargassaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calamaria coromandelina include fucoidan, sulfated polysaccharides, and various phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Calamaria coromandelina include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
